entry.title: Re: BAPI_SHIPMENT_CHANGE clean core alternative?
entry.content:
It is really a strategic decision.
There will not be released options for LE-TRA and it is unlikely that they will come in the future like @Dominik_Tylczynski indicated.
That leads to the conclusion that there is no real clean core option here.

In that case it is just a decision whether to use the known BAPI, thus leave it as is.
Alternatively you could make a wrapper and release that one, in my opinion this does not make it better for your situation.

Yes in general RAP, API, classes take priority.
But if there is currently no standard alternatives towards clean core there is nothing we can do in these situations other than keeping a list of these things and check post upgrades if there is an alternative in the newer release.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One (SQL) – Unique Users with Assigned L...
entry.content:
1. Is there any SQL table where license details are stored? - NO

  2. Can license details be extracted directly through a query? - NO

  3. Or is this information available only via License Administration / License Server? - NO

  4. What is the recommended approach to get a unique user-wise license list? - EXCEL

Excel file with license descriptions and formulas to determine their availability and allocation according to the database.
Take the license description from the license allocation window and create a sheet with rows for users and license type, and columns for the databases where you create that user and grant access.

Note that assigning licenses to a demo database and then deleting the database without removing the license from the user will result in license loss, which can only be recovered by re-importing the license file.

entry.title: Picking a whole HU with mixed batches
entry.content:
We have a requirement to pick and pack HUs that we have in inventory. All of our products are batch managed. The picking and packing works well when the HUs have only one batch. Meaning, the source HU becomes the destination HU during WH task creation, as this is a HU WH Task. However there's a problem with HUs that have more than one batch because in this case the system creates a product WH task and as such the source HU is not set as the destination HU. And even during WH task confirmation we cannot enter the destination HU being the same as the source HU. If we do this we get error /SCWM/L3893 "Destination HU same as source HU only allowed for HU takeover". And if we try ticking the flag "HU Withdrawal" we get error message /SCWM/L3859 "Withdraw from HU XXXX only possible if total amount of HU is withdrawn". So either way we are stuck.

Does anybody experienced it before? In our case we must use the same handling unit number

entry.title: Re: Create a New Field in BCUI
entry.content:
Hi @bbknrt ,

 

In BCUI, expand compInfo and select Configure New Country to set up a country‑specific field. Since a Boolean data type isn’t available, and only String, Date, Long, and Double custom fields are supported, you can create a String custom field and link it to a Yes/No picklist using the ‘Type of Reference Object’ option.

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Re: how to change Gl accounts for journal entry type
entry.content:
Good day @jerrysam 

Thank you for your question.

You can align the company code 33xx with company code 10xx, but only through the available public cloud configurations, not through classic SPRO customising which is restricted. The public cloud typically handles this by checking and adjusting:

  • Automatic Account Determination for the G/L account. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/89f2a87accc849d4b5682feb619ce9ad.html  and https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/ycoa/account-determination for more details.
  • Functional area logic, which is often from G/L master, cost element context, substitution and default assignment logic depending on your setup.

If company code 10xx and 33xx post to different functional areas for the same CBXN scenario, this can often be linked to one or more of these differences:

  • Different account determination configuration
  • Different sales org, chart of accounts, and account assignment group combination
  • Different G/L master settings
  • Different functional area setup
  • Different master data used by the posting.

Also, CBXN is a free goods related item category used in S4H Cloud sales scenarios, so the item category itself can be part of the context, but the final FI posting is still driven by your configuration and derivation logic.

This blog provides some insights: Item category determination in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

A recommended approach is:

  • Before changing anything, compare a posted example from 10xx and 33xx in both company codes first. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/a570b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html for more details.
  • In your billing 33xx document, identify which account key was used, which fields were used for determination and which account 51301xxxx was selected instead of 51001xxx
  • Check the relevant account determination setting in your PC configuration are. Compare chart of accounts, sales org, customer account assignment group, material account assignment group, etc.
  • Functional area may not always come from the same point as the G/L account. Even if you align G/L account, you still need to verify why 606x is used in 33xx while 60xx is used in 10xx.

Question: what happens if you change the settings?

Answer: future posting for the same key combination will use the new G/L account or logic. New billing documents will follow the updated logic. Documents you already posted will not be changed automatically, sales scenarios that use the same key combination might be impacted. Therefore, test in Q system first with the same CBXN example before moving to production.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Runtime Error BEHAVIOR_MANDATORY_FIELD while creating Classification of Equipment using EML
entry.content:
Hi All, I get the following errror while trying to create Classfication and Characteristic Value. Program keeps complaining about the mandatory CLASSINTERNALID no matter i pass it on line 39. Passing it in %control structure also didn't help .I'm out of options ? any help much appreciated

 

 

 

 

REPORT zcwd_classification.


DATA:
  lt_clfn_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_equipmenttp\_Classification,
  lt_char_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_equipmenttp\\equipmentclassificationclass\_characteristic,
  lt_val_create  TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_equipmenttp\\equipmentclassfctnclasscharc\_value.



*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 1️ Level 1: Classification assignment for Equipment
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
lt_clfn_create = VALUE #(
  (
    %tky-equipment       = '000000001000037994'
    %tky-validityenddate = '99991231'

    %target = VALUE #( (
        %cid            = 'NEW_CLFN_1'
        classtype       = '002'
        classinternalid = '0000000017'

        %control-classtype       = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
        %control-classinternalid = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      ) )
  )
).


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 2️ Level 3: Characteristic under Classification
*    
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
lt_char_create = VALUE #(
  (
    %cid_ref             = 'NEW_CLFN_1'          " link to classification assignment
    %tky-equipment       = '000000001000037994'
    %tky-classinternalid = '0000000017'
    %tky-classtype       = '002'

    %target = VALUE #( (
        %cid            = 'NEW_CHAR_1'
        charcinternalid = '0000000937'
        %control-charcinternalid = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      ) )
  )
).


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 3️ Level 4: Characteristic Value
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
lt_val_create = VALUE #(
  (
    %cid_ref = 'NEW_CHAR_1'            " link to characteristic

    %target = VALUE #( (
        %cid        = 'VALUE_CID_1'
        charcvalue  = '1'
        %control-charcvalue = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      ) )
  )
).


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 4️ Execute Deep CREATE
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_equipmenttp
  ENTITY equipment
    CREATE BY \_classification FROM lt_clfn_create
  ENTITY equipmentclassificationclass
    CREATE BY \_characteristic FROM lt_char_create
  ENTITY equipmentclassfctnclasscharc
    CREATE BY \_value FROM lt_val_create
  FAILED   DATA(lt_failed)
  REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 5️ Check for errors
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
IF lt_failed IS NOT INITIAL OR lt_reported IS NOT INITIAL.
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_failed ).
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_reported ).
  RETURN.
ENDIF.


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 6️ Commit
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
COMMIT ENTITIES
  RESPONSE OF i_equipmenttp
  FAILED   DATA(lt_commit_failed)
  REPORTED DATA(lt_commit_reported).

IF lt_commit_failed IS INITIAL.
  WRITE: / 'Success: Classification, characteristic, and value created'.
ELSE.
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_commit_failed ).
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_commit_reported ).
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: PR not visible under purchase data tab in CJ20N
entry.content:
Key checks you need to make

Material Master Settings
Ensure the material has a valid procurement type (F, E, or X) and MRP views maintained. If the material is project stock relevant, check that the special stock indicator (Q) is set correctly.

Project/Network Settings
In CJ20N, confirm that the network activity is released and has the correct control key (procurement allowed). The “Res./Purc. immediately” flag should trigger PR creation, but only if the network is released and dates are valid.

MRP Controller / Plant Assignment
The material must be assigned to the correct plant and MRP controller. If the plant is missing or inconsistent, PR creation will fail silently.

Availability of Procurement Parameters
Check transaction OPSN (parameters for network procurement). Ensure the settings allow automatic PR creation for your network type.

Authorization
Sometimes PR creation fails due to missing authorization for the user to create PRs in the background.

Log / Error Messages
Run CJ20N → Edit → Generate Purchase Requisition manually and check the log. If it says “0 entries were changed,” it means the system did not find eligible components (often due to missing procurement type or incorrect assignment).

entry.title: Joule Studio: Leveraging Agents, Joule Skills, and Document Grounding to extend your use case
entry.content:
Joule Studio: Leveraging Agents, Joule Skills, and Document Grounding to extend your use case

Pre-requisites:

  • Joule Studio
  • Joule Base
  • For document grounding: AI Core (extended plan), AI Launchpad, Object Store

Joule Studio, part of SAP Build, enables developers to extend Joule with Agent Builder and Skills using a low-code/no-code. It provides seamless integration with SAP and Non-SAP data.

With Joule Studio, I created custom agent such that users can simply ask Joule to create a service order whenever an issue arises. Based on the instructions, Joule automatically gather the details, classify the issue based on the company policy using document grounding and create the order in S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

In this blog post, we’ll explore how you can take Joule further with Joule Studio by integrating agents, skills, and document grounding that make your application smarter and more dynamic.

 

1. Setup

Agent Builder

Ensure that you have AI Agent Builder activated. You'll need to toggle the 

 

Document Grounding

This is an amazing blog written by Daniel on setting up Document Grounding in Joule Studio: link

Make sure the extensibility_user role is assigned to your account to access the document grounding feature in Joule Studio.

When new files are added to the S3 folder, you can trigger a manual sychronisation to refresh and ground the documents.

 

Application Setup

To connect your application data to Joule Studio, you can either set up an MCP server or use the BTP Destination Service, depending on your integration needs.

Both options provide seamless connectivity, but the choice depends on the specific use case and the systems you're integrating with Joule Studio.

In this project, I’m using destination service.

In BTP Destination Service, ensure that you include the additional properties listed for it to appear in Joule Studio:

  • sap.processautomation.enabled: true
  • sap.build.usage: odata.gen
  • sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled: true

 

2. Use Actions Project in Joule Studio to encapsulate the API actions

Once the BTP Destination is setup, it will appear in the OData Destination and you can create the Actions Project.

Once the actions project is setup, release and publish it. 

 

3. Create new Joule Skill Project

Within a project, you can setup multiple skills and agents.

Skills are predefined, deterministic workflows that execute single, atomic operations with known inputs and outputs. They function as rule-based tasks and perform repetitive operations without requiring reasoning.  For example, a skill in this project is to get information based on a service order ID.  

Agents are autonomous, multi-tool systems that solve complex problems through dynamic reasoning, planning, and execution. Agents leverage large language models for reasoning, decision-making, and planning, while dynamically selecting and executing the right tools based on the specific context.

 

How Skills and Agents Work Together

Agent Planning: The agent analyzes the user request and determines which skills or tools are needed to accomplish the task.

Skill Execution: The agent invokes relevant Joule skills to perform specific operations like data retrieval or process execution.

Result: The agent combines outputs from multiple skills to provide comprehensive solutions and recommendations

 

Create a Skill

Provide a clear and detailed description of the scenario you want the skill to handle – the more specific you are, the better the outcome will be.

Add “Call Action” to pass the necessary fields to the API to trigger the process.

Add the variables to be passed to the API.

In my project, I have created multiple skills and added them as tools to my agent.

Demo sample:

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, extending Joule Studio to connect with your application data through the MCP server, BTP Destination Service  or API connections, enables seamless integration with external systems. By developing Joule skills and agents, leveraging document grounding and other tools, you can enhance Joule's capabilities and integrate it with your applications.

 

Check out the following resources to help you get started:
Activate Joule Studio

Developer’s Tutorial: Use Joule Studio to Create an Agent

Document Grounding in SAP Joule Studio

entry.title: Business Data Validation in SAP’s Data Transformation Journey: Comparative Overview of LTVF and DTV
entry.content:
Data is at the center of every SAP transformation. Whether the journey involves a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA, a landscape consolidation, data carve-out or a selective data transition, one question always remains critical: 

How do we ensure that business data is complete, accurate, and consistent after transformation? 

Manual reconciliation across systems, company codes, plants, and financial reports is not scalable in modern projects. To address this, SAP provides structured validation approaches that automate data checks and reduce testing effort.  

SAP transformation scenarios like system conversions, landscape consolidations, or selective migrations generate large volumes of business data that must move from source to target system without any scope for loss of data or inconsistency. Manual reconciliation of hundreds of reports is not only time consuming but also error prone. Automated frameworks help teams verify that migrated data aligns with expectations before commitment to production, thus reducing project risk and effort. 

Two key tool-based validation approaches used in SAP transformation programs are: 

  • Landscape Transformation Validation Framework (LTVF) 
  • Data Transition Validation (DTV) 

This blog explains what each approach is, how it works, and where it brings value within SAP transformation initiatives. 

Landscape Transformation Validation Framework (LTVF) 

The Landscape Transformation Validation Framework (LTVF) is a structured validation framework designed to support complex transformation scenarios. It is particularly relevant when data is not simply moved, but restructured, merged, split, or reorganized across systems and organizational units. 

Purpose of LTVF 

LTVF helps reduce test effort and downtime during transformation projects by automating the extraction, comparison, and evaluation of business data before and after transformation activities. 

It is commonly used in scenarios such as: 

  • Selective data transition projects 
  • S/4HANA implementation or conversion programs 
  • Mergers and acquisitions 
  • System consolidations or carve-outs 
  • Chart of accounts harmonization 
  • Organizational restructuring (plants, company codes, controlling areas) and several other scenarios

In these cases, validation is not always a simple “before equals after” check. Data may be aggregated, remapped, or reorganized according to defined transformation rules. LTVF supports this level of complexity. 

How LTVF Works

LTVF operates as a configurable validation framework. The typical process includes: 

1. Defining validation objects 
These may include SAP reports (ALV outputs), table contents, multiple tables join contents, text-based reports, custom reports, custom tables extractions etc. 

2. Setting filters and parameters 
For example, validating with respect to dates, fiscal years or specific organizational units like company codes, or plants etc. 

3. Customer Tailored Scenarios 

  • Full table or selective fields are supposed to be compared. 
  • Cross-table comparison between source and target landscapes. For Example: KONV to be considered from source and PRCD_ELEMENTS to be compared in the target. 
  • Ensure Negative testing by enabling a result format to hold the out-of-scope records. 
  • As it provides field level comparison, even a space of difference can be identified. 
  • Also has the flexibility to accept expected differences, compare substrings, concatenations, tolerances and much more 

4. Applying business logic 
LTVF allows the definition of transformation aware rules, for example: 

  • Renumbering certain fields involved during migration 
  • Aggregation logic after company code mergers 
  • Mapping validations after chart of account changes 
  • Tolerance thresholds for acceptable variances, etc. 
  • In case of consolidation scenarios, a merged result may be required 
  • Any custom logics implemented 

5. Automated comparison and result evaluation 
The framework highlights deviations and allows structured analysis across validation cycles. Because it supports rule-based interpretation of transformed data, LTVF can validate outcomes that are not expected to be identical at field level but must still align from a business perspective. 

Key Benefits of LTVF 

  • Significant reduction in manual reconciliation effort 
  • Structured validation across multiple transformation cycles 
  • Reduced downtime during cutover 
  • Support for complex restructuring logic 
  • Provides a tailored validation approach with respect to each customer 
  • Applicability across master data, transactional data, and configuration data 
  • Works seamlessly with a huge volume of data. 
  • High level statistic report will be available with the tool. 
  • Supports multiple test cycles before go-live 

Data Transition Validation (DTV) 

The Data Transition Validation (DTV) tool is SAP’s standard solution for automated data comparison during system conversions, selective transitions, and system upgrades. DTV is widely used in projects moving to SAP S/4HANA, especially in system conversion, upgrades and lean selective data transition scenarios. 

Purpose of DTV 

DTV is designed to validate that business data remains consistent before and after a system transition. It provides a structured way to: 

  • Capture data snapshots from a source system 
  • Capture corresponding data snapshots from a target system 
  • Automatically compare and highlight differences 
  • Handles large volumes of data 

How DTV Works 

The DTV process typically follows these steps: 

  1. Creation of a validation project 
    A project is defined in the DTV tool, specifying scope and validation objects. 
  2. Selection of validation content 
    SAP delivers predefined validation report content covering key functional areas such as Finance, Materials Management, Sales etc. Customers can also configure custom ALV reports and transparent table comparisons. 
  3. Parameter definition 
    Business filters are applied — for example: Company code, Fiscal year, Date range 
  4. Snapshot extraction 
    Data is extracted from the source system before transition and from the target system after transition. 
  5. Automated comparison and analysis 
    The tool identifies mismatches and provides structured output to support resolution. 

DTV focuses on structured, repeatable comparison cycles, helping teams validate results quickly and consistently across test iterations. 

Key Benefits of DTV 

  • Automated comparison reduces manual validation workload 
  • Pre-delivered SAP validation content accelerates setup 
  • Clear discrepancy identification supports rapid issue resolution 
  • Supports multiple test cycles before go-live 

DTV is especially valuable in system conversion projects where the expectation is that business results should match before and after technical transition. 

Validation as a Strategic Enabler 

Both LTVF and DTV reflect a broader evolution in SAP transformation programs: validation is no longer a last-minute activity. Instead, it is a strategic enabler that: 

  • Improves project transparency 
  • Reduces operational risk 
  • Shortens cutover windows 
  • Builds stakeholder confidence 

As SAP landscapes grow more complex, automated validation frameworks become essential components of transformation architecture rather than optional add-ons. 

 An Overview Comparison 

 

Criteria 

LTVF  

(Landscape Transformation Validation Framework) 

DTV  

(Data Transition Validation) 

Primary Purpose 

Validation for complex landscape and structural transformations 

Validation for system conversions and technical transitions 

Typical Use Case 

SDT, Consolidations, carve-outs, mergers, restructuring etc. 

System conversion or system upgrade 

SAP S/4HANA Conversion 

Can support all including complex selective transitions  

Supported for System Conversion from SAP ECC 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA 2020+ 

SAP S/4HANA Upgrade 

Supported 

Supported for upgrade from S/4HANA 1709+ to higher release 

Lean Selective Data Transition (SDT) 

Strong fit when complex changes apply 

Supported when using Business Transformation Cockpit (BTC) 

NZDT (Near Zero Downtime Technology) 

Supported 

Not supported 

Handling Aggregation / Merging 

Supported 

Limited 

Handling Renumbering / Mapping 

Extensive rule support 

Limited 

Tolerance Thresholds/Acceptance 

Supported 

Limited 

Cross-Table Validation 

Strong capability 

Basic table/ALV comparison 

Pre-delivered SAP Validation Content 

Yes (Reference Catalog is provided for different scenarios) 

Yes (Finance, MM, SD, etc.) 

Multi-System Validation 

Strong capability 

Primarily source → target 

Complexity Handling 

High-complexity transformations 

Medium complexity, standardized scenarios 

Implementation Effort 

Higher setup effort, tailored design 

Faster setup for limited standard content 

 Consultant Support 

From the time of signing until go-live 

From the time of signing until the first test cycle. 

Final Thoughts 

Landscape transformations and system conversions require more than technical delivery; they demand structured assurance that business data remains correct and reliable. 

  • LTVF provides a flexible, rule-driven framework suited for all scenarios including complex restructuring and multi-system transformation scenarios. 
  • DTV offers a standardized and automated validation mechanism tailored for system conversions, upgrades and lean selective data transitions, particularly in SAP S/4HANA projects. 

By integrating the right validation approach early in the project lifecycle, organizations can significantly reduce testing effort, minimize downtime, and ensure a smoother path to go-live. 

Some reference links: 

3271210 - BDV - Business Data Validation Service - SAP for Me 

LTVF: 

3271502 - LTVF - Landscape Transformation Validation Framework - SAP for Me 

Reducing Test-Effort and Downtime using the valida... - SAP Community 

DTV:

entry.title: Re: POs are not transferred to accruals engine for SES ac...
entry.content:
Answer to your questions

Should SES be raised via Fiori app F2027 for SES accruals to trigger?
Yes. SES accruals are only supported for Lean Services SES created in F2027, not for classic ML81N.

Can F2027 SES be created against a PO raised in ME21N?
Yes, as long as the PO is for Lean Services (item category D, service maintained). The SES created in F2027 will then be picked up by ACEPOSTINGRUN.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Reference Check Invoice Number MIRO & C...
entry.content:
Dear @Nagadatt,

To turn the duplicate check into a hard error, you must do both:

Point 1. Make sure the system is actually detecting the duplicate (key fields)

In CBC activity Set Check for Duplicate Invoices ID 103518 ensure your company code has the check indicators set at least Company Code + Reference + Invoice Date. If the second invoice differs in one of the checked fields very often invoice date, it may not be treated as a duplicate

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb525a45b55f4a1c9bb32c254affeeec/c874994366d94528a23eabb26982bb0c.html

Point 2. Convert the duplicate message to Error

In CBC ID 102091 Define Message Types for Area M8 set the Customer message type to E (Error) for the duplicate messages relevant to MIRO typically M8108

IMPORTANT: You cannot change the Standard message type column only the Customer column is meant for you to maintain

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3637235

Additional information

SAP published guidance for Public Cloud is that F5117 message control can’t be modified to an error in Create Incoming Invoices (FB60) app

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3135711

Also

If you are posting invoices via API/IDoc, SAP states the duplicate check is an online/UI function and isn’t executed for those automatic postings

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3687026

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SF EC MDF UI Hacks: When RBP Isn’t Enough to Hide Fields/Groups
entry.content:
Problem Statement:

Ever tried to hide a field in MDF UI and wondered why it magically shows up for the employee during workflow approval? 😅

Yep, it’s a classic SuccessFactors “feature” you hide it in RBP, but the moment the record is pending approval, employees get VIP access to your secret fields. And if an approver sends it back? Boom! They can see and even update fields they shouldn’t even touch. 😬

Have you ever tried to change the visual display of groups and fields on the UI based on the logged-in user and couldn’t figure out a way to achieve it other than using RBP?

With standard we can't get the Login user() function in the UI rules. But there is a trick

Solution:

So how do we fix this? Drumroll… A simple paradiddle. Lol. 🥁

Enter the OnLoad Rule!

Instead of relying on RBP alone, we can use UI OnLoad rules to control what the user sees. Here’s the trick:

  1. Create a dummy variable field in your MDF (let’s call it ShowHideFlag).
  2. Write an OnLoad rule:

If login user = Your MDF.externalCode,

  then set ShowHideFlag = 1

        Note: The externalCode has to be of Type User.

              The ShowHideFlag has to be of Type Number.

  1. Then, create UI rules based on that variable:
    • If ShowHideFlag = 1 → Show/Hide fields, sections, or groups
    • Else → Show/Hide fields, sections, or groups

How it helps?

💡 Basically, the UI now knows who’s looking at it and can hide/show fields dynamically. No sneaky employee access allowed!

With this approach, you gain better control over your MDF UI behavior, even during workflow scenarios where RBP alone falls short. A small OnLoad rule can make a big difference in protecting data visibility and ensuring the right user sees the right fields at the right time.

If you found this useful, please give it a 👍 and let me know in the comments if it helped.

I’d also love to hear your thoughts on any alternative solutions you’ve implemented!

entry.title: Default Cost Center for Postings for Statistical Project
entry.content:
Hi gurus

I have created a statistical project where I have added a Default Cost Center for Postings, then I have created more WBS and released the project. I add a budget to that cost center so that it works correctly.

The problem arises when I try to place a purchase order (through the me21n app). There is no problem with any WBS in the first order, but if I try to change the Default Cost Center for Postings in the project to another cost center with a budget and place the purchase order again, it only takes the main WBS with that cost center.

In other words, the sub-items do not work with that new cost center, and an error pops up saying that the previous cost center has no budget (which makes me think that it is still linked to the cost center of the project release).

Is this behavior correct, or am I missing a step?

Best regards, I hope you can resolve my question.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting - Using Automatic Posting Rules for Consolidation of Investments
entry.content:
Hi Madhu,

There is not yet a tool to migrate reclassification rules to new posting rules. Customer can of course continue to use existing reclassification rules. 

The rules for net income calculation and intercompany eliminations are delivered in 2602 release. See pages 97-98 in the Group Reporting 2602 Feature Deck  

Customers that have activated Group Reporting (Scope Item 1SG) before release 2602 can start to use and adjust these rules flexibly whenever this fits their plans.

The rules for consolidation of investments are planned to be delivered later in 2026.

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Grégoire

entry.title: How to set up workflow action "Create Jira Issue" in SAP Signavio Process Governance
entry.content:
Stop juggling platforms! SAP Signavio Process Governance now integrates with Jira, enabling you to automatically create issues directly from your workflows. Released in November 2025, this integration allows you to reduce manual effort, ensure consistent issue creation through predefined fields, and keep teams focused on their core tasks. By simplifying logging and tracking issues in Jira, the integration improves visibility and coordination—so governance activities stay efficient, structured, and transparent. 

This blog post will walk you through the setup and execution steps from two perspectives: 

  1. Jira administrator configuration of Jira credentials 
  2. The modeler embedding the Jira action in the workflow 



Admin User Flow (Jira) 

To enable the Jira integration, a Jira administrator must create an API token that allows SAP Signavio Process Governance to authenticate securely with Jira. 

Prerequisites: 

  • Jira Administrator access is needed 

Jira Administrator – Prepare Access

  1. Navigate to Atlassian Account → Security. 
  2. Create an API Token
    1. Give your API token a name that describes what it does.
    2.  Select an expiration date for the API token. 
    3. Token expiration is 1 to 365 days. (Atlassian sends email reminders to the token owner) 
    4. Click Create.  
    5. Select Copy to clipboard, then paste the token to your script, or save it somewhere safe.

       

Jira Project Access Setup 

To configure project settings and manage workflows in Jira, the user must have administrator access to the project. 

Prerequisites 

  • The user must be added to the Jira project. 
  • The user must have the Project Administrator role assigned. 
Steps 
  1. Navigate to Project settings in your Jira project.

     

    1. From the left-hand menu, select Users and roles. 

       

  2. Review the list of users assigned to the project.

    1.   Ensure the required user is assigned the Administrators role. 

    2.   If needed, add the user to the project and assign the Administrators role. 

SAP Signavio Process Governance Administrator 

Prerequisites: 

  • Jira Token 
  • Workspace administrator access rights 
Steps 
  1. Create a new Configuration in SAP Signavio Process Governance 
    1. Open the Organization Settings by clicking on the User Profile menu in the top right corner of the header area. 
  2. Create Credentials 
    1. Click on “Credentials” to create a new set of credentials to authenticate against Jira
    2. Click on “Create credential” and enter the parameters from the Jira App: 
      1. Username – ex. Jira  
      2. Name - name describing the credential 
      3. Secret Value - token generated from Jira application

 

You can find more information in SAP Help. 

  1.  Configure Jira Create Issue Activity 
    1. Go to “Activities”, select the “Jira - Create Issue” activity and click “Add configuration

       

    2. Configure the activity:
      1. Configuration Name: name describing the configuration. 
      2. Configuration Description: Add description to the configuration (e.g. New create issue action) 
      3. Jira server URL: add link to Jira server 
      4. Jira API credentials: select credentials created in second step 
      5. Project Key: list of Jira projects within a Jira workspace. This will be the workspace; issues created will be saved. 

You can find more information for the remaining fields in SAP Help. 

Test, enable and save the configuration. 

 

Modeler User Flow 

 

 Prerequisites: 

  • Jira create Issue Activity is configured and enabled 
Steps:
  1.   Create a new process/workflow with a form trigger 

     

    1. Add External Activity: Go to “Actions” tab and select “External Activities”. Select activity “Jira - Create Issue” and  the configuration you created before:
       

       

  2. Configure activity in workflow: Inputs 
    1. The Inputs section defines the information required to create a new Jira issue. Each field  maps directly to a Jira issue attribute and ensures the issue is created with the correct  context and ownership. 
      1. Assignee: Choose who will be responsible for handling the issue. You can either select a user directly from the list of available project members in Jira or use a label field that can later be reused to select the user in the Trigger form.
      2. Description: Add a detailed explanation of the issue, including its requirements, scope, or any relevant background information. Alternatively, you can use a label field that can be repurposed later for user selection in the Trigger form
      3. Issue Type: Specifies the type of issue to be created in Jira, such as Epic, Story, or Task, based on the project configuration. 
      4. Priority: Determines the urgency of the issue, using the priority levels defined in the Jira project (for example, Low, Medium, or High).
      5. Reporter: Identify who is reporting or creating the issue. This can be done by selecting a user directly from the dropdown, or by using a label field that can be reused later in the Trigger form.
      6. Summary: Provide a short, clear title that quickly communicates what the issue is about. As an alternative, you may use a label field that can later be repurposed for user selection in the Trigger form.


        You can find more information in SAP Help how to configure the activity 
  3. Configure activity in workflow: Outputs 
    1. Create the following output fields to capture the response returned by Jira after issue  creation: 
      1. Error message Displays details, such as incorrect field values, to explain why a Jira issue could not be created. A successful Jira issue creation triggers a success message output. 
      2. Issue Key Displays a unique code identifier for the Jira issue. This consists of a two-digit project key followed by a hyphen and a sequential number. 
      3. Issue URL Consists of a web address that links to a specific task or issue within a Jira project. 
      4. Success Confirms that a Jira issue was created successfully. An unsuccessful creation triggers and error message output. 

         

  4. Check results 
    1. Add a “User Task” action after the Jira activity to check the result and display the URL of the created Issue. 

       

  5. Publish the process 

    1. After completing all required changes and reviewing the workflow configuration, publish the process to make it active. 

      Click the Publish to run this process button in the top-right corner to save your updates  and make the process available for execution. 

       

Further Resources: 

  • SAP Help Portal: Enabling Jira - Create Issue 
  • SAP Enable Click-Thru Demos: 
    • Administrator 
    • Process Owner

entry.title: Re: Add Attachment to ouptut in Sales Quotation

entry.title: Re: Default Cost Center for Postings for Statistical Project
entry.content:
What you are seeing is actually standard behavior in S/4HANA Public Cloud projects:

When you release a project with a default cost center for postings, that default is inherited by the WBS elements at the time of release.

Changing the default cost center later in the project definition does not retroactively update the cost center assignment of existing WBS elements. Each WBS element keeps the cost center it was originally linked to.

That is why your sub‑items still point to the old cost center and trigger the “no budget” error — they are still technically assigned to the previous cost center, even though you changed the project’s default.

What you can do

Check WBS Master Data
Go into each WBS element and verify the “Responsible Cost Center” field. Update it to the new cost center if required.

Mass Change Option
In Public Cloud, you can use the “Project Control” or “Project Builder” apps to perform mass changes on WBS elements. This is the recommended way if you have many WBS elements.

Budget Assignment
Ensure the new cost center has budget assigned. After updating the WBS elements, the purchase order will consume against the correct cost center budget.

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?

Ensure transport requests are released, then run “SMLT → Reinstall Language” to update all texts and translations. Some standard transactions may still need manual adjustments if previous install was partial.

entry.title: SAC Planning and Datasphere: How to use Audit Logs for Analysis and Recovery
entry.content:
Introduction

Tracing published data changes in SAC is not an easy task. The default "Data Changes" application in SAC (Security -> Data Changes) is a list report that shows the full granularity of the data. Functionalities like in the data analyzer to drag and drop columns to analyze the data changes are missing. Furthermore, the data changes cannot be used to roll back the published plan data to a specific point in time. In an earlier blog post I compared the SAC model with the write optimized ADSO in SAP BW, not having the request information and inherited delta mechanism.

In this blog post I will share a solution for better analysis and recovery via change logs taking advantage of the newest seamless planning features. This solution does not necessarily require seamless planning, but I will go into detail later.

Architecture

Remarks regarding DPA / Remote Table:

  • DPA with CDI Adapter is required for real time aquisition
  • Audit Logs for a specific model can be aquired in Datasphere via CDI connection type
  • Performance of remote table is crucial - consider aquiring only the latest changes via remote table and persist older changes via data flow in Datasphere. These considerations heavily depend of your requirements, model and data volume.
  • Enforcing a dynamic filter push down via remote table on the Audit_Time field was only possible by adding bind_as_value function in the where clause: Query Parameterization: BIND_AS_PARAMETER and BIND_AS_VALUE | SAP Help Portal -> when working with remote tables always check out the actual statement that's being pushed down
  • If you don't have seamless planning yet, the audit data could be loaded via import job to SAC instead of using a live version - this would require the execution of the import job to have a look at the latest data changes
  • Latency of the view on the remote table is about 3 seconds also for empty audit tables - i guess, that's the communication overhead. Good news is that a view with filter push down on huge audit tables has a similar performance

Analyzing the data in SAC

First, I will show you a simple story for planning CostCenter - GLAccount combinations. As you can see, CostCenter A and C have a total of 200 each:

When I look at the audit log story, I can see that the sum of the delta values equals the result in the plan story:

Every dimension is available as filter criteria. For consistency reasons the "SourceVersion" (i.e. the actual version in the plan model) is a single value filter. The "Version" of the audit model is the version "live". That is, because the live connection from Datasphere to SAC is established via an own version. Furthermore, in contrast to the standard application for data changes, we have now the ability to use the new "Quick Builder" in the story to drag and drop dimensions to further analyze the data changes. We could also apply hierarchies to answer business related questions like: "Why and when did my EBIT change?"

Undo data changes / recover plan data in SAC

A critical incident would be the unintended deletion of the whole version data. In this small example I deleted the "public.Budget" data for the whole model. In the audit story the deletions are visible:

Since the audit data is in a SAC model now, the delta records can be used for a cross model copy step to undo this data change. One way to achieve it is by appending the inverted delta value in the cross model copy step. In this example model I build a cross model copy step with all possible filter criteria as parameters (also, for instance, the audit time). Those input parameters are bound to the story filters in this specific story. So, by filtering to the specific audit date in this example, the data action ("Undo Selection") - inheriting the cross model copy step - is prefilled:

After execution of the data action and reset of the story filter, the inverted delta bookings can be seen in the audit log story:

Also in the plan story, the data is visible again!

Remarks regarding cross model copy step:

  • The relevant dimensions (here: CostCenter, GLAccount) should be public in both models
  • The date dimension, by its private nature, should be generated identically in the audit model as in the plan model
  • Theoretically, fact and corresponding master data could be deleted in the plan model as part of a housekeeping task. Keep in mind that the audit logs should be deleted for those combinations aswell to avoid warning messages.
  • If you check out the data action prompt, you can see that the "SourceVersion" and the "TargetVersion" (i.e. the version in the plan model) are two different prompts. It probably never makes sence to recover data to another "TargetVersion" as the "SourceVersion". To eliminate potential risks here, the "TargetVersion" prompt could be filled via script.

Towards a Reliable Change‑Recovery Process in SAC

SAC’s open, self‑service-oriented planning allows users to adjust productive data directly, which speeds up planning but also introduces a risk to data quality, especially when self‑service stories are used in production. In our project, this led to situations where entire versions were deleted unintentionally or values were spread across all dates due to a single user action. Recovery required manual corrections based on audit logs or deletion of unintended data.

Live Versions improve the situation by capturing delta values and deletions more consistently and reducing gaps that previously occurred through import jobs. This strengthens the audit trail and provides a more reliable basis for analyzing changes and restoring data. However, the audit log is not guaranteed to remain perfectly synchronized with the planning model (entries may be missing or deleted), so it should always be viewed as a time‑limited recovery source.

The approach described in this blog uses audit data in Datasphere and SAC to make changes analyzable across dimensions and to restore data selectively using inverted deltas. This creates a practical, repeatable recovery mechanism that reduces the risks of self‑service planning in production while preserving SAC’s flexibility. It covers not only new and modified records but also deleted ones.

And for anyone at SAP working on seamless planning: the recent developments are very encouraging! Making audit logs natively accessible in Datasphere would further strengthen performance and simplify recovery scenarios.

entry.title: Naming/Set up company code ID
entry.content:
Dear Community,

since last year SAP changed the logic regarding company code ID from numerical to alphernumeric.

Meaning Company Code set up from 1010 to 10DE for e.g.

Based on our experience this logic is not aligned within the whole SAP landscape, meaning within the Starter System for e.g. alphernumeric (10DE) is mandatory but within the Customer System it was still possible to go ahead with the old logic (1010)

Has this already been aligned or is it still the case, that both ways for company code set up are possible?

The only reference we found at SAP about this topic is from 2024:

3378323 - Naming Convention of Company Codes - SAP for Me

Thx & BR

Marcel

entry.title: CK2027 Error When Marking Standard Cost Estimates in F1865 - Organizational Measure Not Allowed for
entry.content:
## Issue Description

**Error Message:** CK2027
**Error Text:**
> "In Legal Valuation the following occurred: You are trying to mark materials for period 002/2026 and company code 1510 in ledger 0L, although marking has not been allowed for this."

**System:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
**App Used:** F1865 - Manage Costing Runs - Estimated Costs
**Step Failed:** Marking step in costing run execution
**Business Context:** Standard cost estimate marking for period 002/2026

---

## Root Cause

The organizational measure (organizational standard) has not been enabled for **marking standard cost estimates** for the following combination:
- Company Code: 1510
- Ledger: 0L (Leading Ledger)
- Period: 002/2026
- Valuation Type: Legal Valuation

---

## Solution

### Step 1: Navigate to Organizational Standards App

**App ID to search:** Look for organizational standards/measures app (likely something like F2XXX or similar)

**Search terms in SAP Fiori Launchpad:**
- "Organizational Standards"
- "Organizational Measure"
- "Manage Organizational Standards"
- "組織標準"

### Step 2: Access the Configuration

Once in the app:

1. Filter/navigate to:
- Company Code: **1510**
- Ledger: **0L**
- Fiscal Year/Period: **002/2026**

2. Look for settings related to **Product Costing** or **Material Price Determination**

### Step 3: Enable Marking Permission

1. Click on **Company Code** or **Valuation View** in the list
2. You'll either see:
- Permitted costing variants (if already enabled), OR
- A request/button to enable organizational measure
3. Enable marking for your **Costing Variant** used in the costing run
4. Ensure **"Allow Marking"** is checked for period 002/2026
5. Save

### Step 4: Retry in F1865

1. Go back to **F1865 - Manage Costing Runs - Estimated Costs**
2. Select your costing run
3. Re-execute the **Marking** step

Error should be resolved.

---

## Additional Info Needed

**Question:** What is the exact Fiori App ID for managing organizational standards in your system? (So I can give you the exact app to use)

entry.title: Re: How in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud legal requirement fo...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, threshold‑based tax rates (like the GST examples you listed for apparel, footwear, and luxury items) are managed through the Advanced Compliance Reporting and Tax Service framework rather than hard‑coding rates into the material master. Here’s how it works conceptually:

How threshold‑based GST rates are handled

Tax Service / Determination
Public Cloud uses the SAP Localization Hub – Tax Service (integrated with S/4HANA Cloud). Tax determination is based on condition records (similar to SD pricing) but extended with tax classification and thresholds.

Condition Technique
You define tax condition types (e.g., GST5, GST12, GST18, GST40). Access sequences can include material tax classification, product category, and transaction value. Thresholds (like Rs. 500, Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,500) are modeled as scale values in the condition record.

Scale‑Based Tax Rates
For apparel: condition record with scale values →

  • Up to Rs. 2,500 → 5%
  • Above Rs. 2,500 → 12% (or 18% in 2025 framework).

For footwear: condition record with scale values →

  • Up to Rs. 500 → 5%
  • Above Rs. 500 → 18%.

Product Allocation / Classification
Materials are assigned to tax categories (e.g., “Apparel,” “Footwear,” “Luxury”). The system reads the transaction value (net price per piece) and applies the correct tax rate based on the scale.

Future Framework (GST 2.0, 2025/26)
New slabs (like 40% for luxury vehicles) are introduced as new condition records with thresholds. You don’t need new material masters — the same product can have multiple tax rates depending on value.

entry.title: Implementing B2B Advisory in SAP Integration Suite for Custom XML EDIFACT Scenario
entry.content:
In many organizations, B2B transactions are not always handled using pure standard formats. Over time, custom XML structures are introduced on top of EDIFACT messages to align with internal processing or EDI broker requirements.

I encountered a similar scenario in one of my recent projects. Through our EDI broker, we were consuming EDIFACT messages in an XML representation. With the upcoming S/4HANA migration, the same integrations needed to be moved to SAP Integration Suite.

Interestingly, the migration itself was not the biggest challenge. The real concern was monitoring and trading partner mantinance.

In our existing SAP PI/PO landscape, we have more than 400 active interfaces processing approximately 200,000+ transactions. Monitoring in such a landscape is already complex. Moving to Integration Suite without a structured monitoring approach would only increase the operational overhead.

Why We Considered B2B Advisory

To address this, we evaluated B2B Advisory capabilities in Integration Suite. The key expectations were:

  • Automatic payload logging retention for a defined number of days
  • Clear identification of trading partner information
  • Agreement-based processing for structured partner management
  • A dedicated monitoring environment for B2B transactions

Another important aspect was our S/4HANA clean core strategy. By leveraging B2B Advisory features properly, we see opportunities to reduce custom logic and keep the core system less cluttered with monitoring-specific enhancements.

Migration Context: From PO Without TPM to Integration Suite
In our SAP PO system, we were not using Trading Partner Management (TPM). As a result, many partner-specific handling rules were built into custom logic. While setting up B2B Advisory in Integration Suite, we started seeing clear structural benefits — especially around agreement handling and partner-based message identification. Even at this in-progress stage, the difference in approach compared to PO is quite visible.

This blog focuses on how I configured B2B Advisory to support our XML-based custom EDIFACT messages. Since we could not directly use the standard packages as-is, I made specific adjustments to adapt them to our scenario. I will walk through:

  • How the standard B2B Advisory packages behave
  • What limitations we observed with custom XML EDIFACT
  • What modifications were required
  • Key learnings during configuration

This is still work in progress, but the insights gained so far are already valuable, especially for teams moving from PI/PO to Integration Suite without prior TPM usage.

  • I have devided thie blog into 3 parts where current blog refers the configuration what we need to do in TPM (Trading partner Management such as Compnay profile, Partner profile, Identifiers, Communication configuration etc.
  • 2nd blog will be based on --> agreements and couple of features i have used.
  • 3rd blog will be how Iflows are configured and customized as per the need.

Quick Concept: How B2B Advisory & Integration works

 Above is the a explanitary flow how messgae processing works with B2B Advisory. Here the brain is TPM Setup and Iflows are executing engine. Let's break down the setup I have created within TPM.

 

TPM Setup for B2B Advisory

As explained in the processing flow, the standard B2B Advisory packages rely heavily on Trading Partner Management (TPM). The iFlow itself does not contain hardcoded partner logic. Instead, it dynamically resolves sender, receiver, and agreement details from TPM configuration at runtime. The TPM setup was structured as follows.

Company

The Company object represents our organization within the B2B landscape. In our case:

  • It reflects our enterprise entity interacting with external partners
  • This acts as the anchor for all agreements.
  • All inbound and outbound B2B agreements are defined relative to this Company.

Important consideration:
We ensured that the company identifiers align with the values extracted from the XML payload. If the identifiers do not match exactly, agreement resolution will fail.

Here I have created 2 identifiers from SAP where 1st "BTPISEDI" is the one i'm using for all outbound from SAP (Idoc always) as this is the port we have defined for IDoc in WE21. and "DS4CLNT120" for all inbound to SAP as that is the logical system for all inbound. How those will be used will be describe in coming setps.

On the continuation on Company profile, we have Identifer where we can add individual identifer and Group Identifier. Group will be used when we have the varite of identifers. 

IdentifierActual value from the payload which will be use to identify the flow
AliasRepresentation of the identifer which will be display at config level
Type SystemType System means the Source Document type in simple language
Scheme Name/CodeKind of the identifer number for internal use eg : Customer Number or Partner Profile
Agency Name/CodeKind of the identidier for the trading partner eg : DUNS number or Sender ID

Within the Trading Partner Management setup, the Systems tab allows us to define communication system details such as IDoc or Process Direct configuration parameters.

However, it is important to understand that these are not actual adapters.

The configurations maintained here do not represent runtime adapters directly. Instead, they act as logical placeholders that the standard B2B package references during execution.

How It Works Conceptually:

In TPM, we define a System for:

  • External partner side
  • Our backend system (e.g., S/4HANA)
  • Type System (System version)

Under the System definition, we maintain adapter-related attributes such as:

  • Endpoint url of the system (internet/On-Prim)
  • Location ID (if applicable)
  • Process Direct url
  • Credentails if application 

These settings are then picked up by the standard B2B iFlow at runtime. The iFlow reads the resolved Agreement --> identifies the associated Systems --> and dynamically maps the configured values into the actual adapter that is connected in the integration flow.

Learning from various hit/try : If under system purpose is maintain as test, then in case of inbound to SAP, Idoc will never post for automatic posting it should be marked at production.

Partner Profile

Next step would be maintain the system and trading partner information. Here 2 section are devided into 2 maintinance. 1st is the Communication Partner (EDI Broker/Trading Partner Connected System/any other middleware) and Trading Partner (your actual customer/vendor).

Quick concept on Communication Partner :

  • protocol supported Process Direct and AS2.
  • only connection details are requried under communication partner.
  • technical placeholder for connection.
  • certificates/signatature (for signing and encrption) are available (mostly used with AS2)

Trading Partner

Same setup what we do in company profile need to be recreate here. Except, we can ignore the communication part (channel details/type system). Just adding the reference of the communication partner is sufficient.

 
 

Here in the identifier for trading partner I have used group of identifiers instead of individual identifier (nothing intresting just because I have tried individual).  These Identifier are used to fetch the correct agreement which will have a better monitoring view per trading partner.

 

How to extract Identifiers

Ok, we have discussed about how to maintain identifer, but how to find the identfier the a little complex. 

  • Company Profile
    • Control Records from Idoc should be the first choice as that have all the Sender/Receiver information such as Logical system, Sender port, Receiver partner profile code etc. 
      • For Company Profile (Outbound) : As generally we have a single company profile so we should choose a value which is common accross all the Outbound flow (Logical system/XML Port are preferable)
      • For Company Profile (Inbound) : Preferable option is the Logical System which we pass in RCVPRN
  • Trading Partner Profile
    • Check the file which you will be consuming and the section where Sender/Receiver information is being transfer 
      StandardSegmentSender Position

      Receiver Position

      EDIFACTUNBS002 (4th element)

      S003 (5th element)

      ANSI X12ISAISA06ISA08
      VDA (Classic)511Pos 4-12Pos 13-21
      EDIWheelBuyer/Seller Party--
      Customized formatSender/Receiver section  

 

Next series of this blog will be on how agreements can be create (will not be too standard as I have faced couple of challange) and additional paramters which are useful. Also, the configuration we made in this part, how these play a valuable role and where these will be visible at runtime. 

link (will be update once published) : Implementing B2B Advisory in SAP Integration Suite for Custom XML EDIFACT Scenarios part - 2

 

Thanks

Aman Sharma

entry.title: Re: Situation Handling for Maintenance Order – Trigger No...
entry.content:
Good day @sriram_00770 

Based on your screenshots, in the standard situation type EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED, the object filters exposed in the app do not include Order Phase and System Status. This is a template limitation in Public Cloud.

See the following references for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/9e5578397eff4a6588eba5fc1f2ddf56.html

The Public Cloud allows you to notify user when maintenance order changes phase in general by using standard situation type Maintenance Order Phase Changed. You can also filter by the available header level attributes and therefore allowing you to somewhat narrow the scope.

The currently exposed filter fields and the details on your screenshot, you cannot directly trigger only for “Post Execution” phase while using the standard condition builder, you cannot filter by CNF/TECO system status in the standard anchor object filters. Therefore, you cannot achieve a clean phase specific trigger with standard configuration alone in this template. A core limitation.

 

Question 1: How can we trigger a situation notification specifically when a maintenance order enters the post-execution stage in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Answer: The standard template can trigger on maintenance order phase change events, but since Order Phase is not exposed as filter, you cannot restrict the trigger to only post execution.

Question 2: Is there any standard workaround or recommended approach to achieve this using Situation Handling?

Answer: Yes, but it is a workaround, not an exact filter-based solution. It would be for you to use EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED and restrict as much as possible with available filters. Then configure recipients, so only production relevant users receive it. A trade off could be that some users may receive notification for multiple phase changes, not only post execution.

Alternatively, you modify your operational process rule by:

  • Triggering on phase changes generally
  • In the notification text, clearly instruct your users to resume production only when the order is in Post Execution/completed maintenance step.
  • Users verify current order phase in the maintenance order app before they carry out any action.

Question 3: Is this a known limitation by design for maintenance orders in Public Cloud?

Answer: Yes. See the following references for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/9e5578397eff4a6588eba5fc1f2ddf56.html

So yes, this is effectively a standard template limitation by design in Public Cloud. If your business needs exact phase filtering, the best path is an SAP feature request to expose Order Phase/System Status as condition fields in the template.

 

Thank you for your question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How to hide "Compensation Structure" object for some countries/legal entities?
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We have a requirement to create a "Compensation Structure" object for a country. The object is created via Manage Data.

This object needs to be displayed on the "Compensation Information" screen in EC and is relevant for only one country. The object has been successfully created and is visible on Compensation Information screen.

As this object is not required for other countries apart from one country, I have tried to write a business rule for hiding this field for "onView" scenario and it works perfectly fine. 

There is also a need to hide the field for other countries for "onInit" scenario and the rule does not seem to work.

This object "Compensation Structure" is a standard out of box object, it also does not allow a custom UI to be created where an "onInit" rule can be applied.

Furthermore, I have had to add this field at the "compinfo" level HRIS element and not at the country-level HRIS element as doing so at the country-level does not allow the "Compensation Structure" object to be reportable. This is product gap duly acknowledged by SAP.

Could you please help suggest how to hide this object for "onInit" scenario where the Compensation Information screen when being rendered first time without any Compensation information values is correctly able to hide this field for all countries minus one.

Any help/suggested is much much appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Gaming
entry.content:
Love that energy 😄  those “random deep dive” hobbies are the best.

I haven’t fallen into the Warhammer 40,000 or Warhammer Age of Sigmar rabbit hole myself (yet), but I totally get the obsession cycle. One week it’s something casual, and the next you’re learning full lore timelines and faction histories at 2 AM.

Funny enough, my current spiral isn’t miniatures it’s the Letter Boxed unlimited word puzzle from The New York Times. It sounds tame compared to Warhammer, but it turns into the same kind of mental rabbit hole:

  • You start “just solving the daily puzzle.”

  • Then you optimize for two-word solutions.

  • Then you track solve times.

  • Then suddenly you’re analyzing letter distribution patterns like it’s tabletop strategy.

Different battlefield, same tactical brain energy 😅

Honestly, there’s something similar about both hobbies:

  • Warhammer → strategic positioning, long-term planning, deep lore.

  • Letter Boxed → constraint-based thinking, pattern recognition, vocabulary strategy.

Both scratch that “I must master this system” itch.

So no painted armies on my desk yet but definitely a growing spreadsheet of word puzzle stats.

What faction (or grand alliance) pulled you in first?

Procedure

Define the depreciation area using the Customizing menu.

--> for the Company code xxxx is no depreciation area 20 maintained. 

Best regards
Thomas

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?
entry.content:
In ST05 (or any transaction code you like), start the menu System > Status > Double-click the GUI Status > menu Goto > Translation and select the translations from German to English. You may also use SE63: in S/4HANA 2023, the main ST05 program is R_ST05_TRACE_MAIN, in SE63 type "T" + Enter in the command field then select LIMU CUAD R_ST05_TRACE_MAIN and language from deDE to enUS. In my "French system", I can see that the translations exist in English but not in French. If the French translations exist in your system, then reset the language buffer (try RSLANG20, then SAPRSEUG). If only the English translations exist, check that the profile parameter zcsa/second_language is set to "E" in RZ11 (if yes, reset the language buffer).

EDIT: you have said "SMLT has not been done correctly", do you mean the log shows error messages or you just don't see few texts translated?
